purpose: Implement a backend policy layer that decides which context sources, CodeCompass/RAG blocks, local files, secrets, memories, artifacts and external knowledge sources may be sent to which worker, runtime target, LLM provider, model scope and tool. This is the backend half of user-configurable data-boundary enforcement.
problem_statement: Worker selection alone is not safe. The system must decide for every context block whether it may be sent to native local worker, OpenCode, Hermes, remote worker, cloud LLM, local LLM, summarizer, reviewer, repair runner, memory writer or tool invocation. Local workers may read much more than cloud/external workers, but writes must still be policy/approval gated.
global_principles: Context access is decided per block, not globally per task.
Source policy, worker policy, runtime policy and model provider policy must all allow a block before it is sent.
Secrets are denied by default, even for local LLMs, unless a narrow non-LLM tool policy explicitly allows access.
Cloud/external workers receive only cloud_allowed, redacted or summarized context.
Local worker read permission is not the same as write permission.
Policy decisions must be traceable: included, redacted, summarized and denied blocks require reason codes.
CodeCompass/RAG retrieval must filter before prompt assembly, not after prompt assembly.
No raw secret, token, .env, private key or credential should appear in prompts, traces, artifacts, memory or diagnostics by default.
non_goals: Do not rely on prompt instructions to stop cloud models from using sensitive context.
Do not give all local workers unrestricted write access.
Do not make cloud_allowed a single global boolean without source-level rules.
Do not trust file path matching alone for security; combine source metadata, classification and redaction.
Do not silently drop context without traceable explanation.
Do not allow UI config to bypass backend validation.

milestones.title: Integration with CodeCompass, RAG and context bundles

tasks.title: Define ContextAccessPolicy schema
tasks.problem: There is no single authoritative policy schema that maps source areas to allowed workers, runtime kinds, model scopes, provider locations and read/write/send permissions.
implementation_notes: Create a strict schema first. Enforcement can be built on top of it.
Use explicit deny-by-default behavior for unmatched sources unless project default policy says otherwise.
Policy should support inheritance: global defaults, project policy, blueprint role policy, task override policy.
acceptance_criteria: ContextAccessPolicy includes policy_id, version, scope, defaults, rules, precedence, created_at, updated_at and validation_state.

Each rule includes id, description, source_match, source_types, sensitivity, allowed_worker_kinds, denied_worker_kinds, allowed_runtime_kinds, denied_runtime_kinds, allowed_model_scopes, denied_model_scopes, allowed_provider_locations, denied_provider_locations, read_allowed, write_allowed, send_allowed, cloud_allowed, external_worker_allowed, redaction_required, summarization_allowed, approval_required and reason_tags.
Valid model scopes include local_model, local_tool_only, private_remote, approved_cloud, public_cloud, none or equivalent enum.
Valid source types include codecompass_code, codecompass_graph, rag_chunk, local_file, secret_file, env_file, memory, artifact, log, config, docs, external_source and user_prompt.
Policy validation rejects rules that allow cloud for sensitivity=secret unless explicitly marked as impossible/invalid or admin override with approval requirement.
Policy validation rejects write_allowed=true when no allowed worker/runtime/tool constraints exist.
Tests cover valid minimal policy, valid strict local policy, invalid secret-to-cloud policy and invalid unrestricted write policy.
anti_patterns: Do not encode this only as ad hoc booleans in task config.
Do not use one global cloud_allowed flag as complete policy.
Do not allow unmatched sensitive sources by default.

tasks.title: Define ContextBlockAccessDecision contract
tasks.problem: The system needs a machine-readable decision for each context block: allow, redact, summarize, deny or require approval.
implementation_notes: Decision should be attachable to ContextEnvelope/ContextBundle metadata and trace.
Keep raw content out of decisions; reference block ids/hashes.
acceptance_criteria: ContextBlockAccessDecision includes block_id, source_ref, matched_rule_ids, decision, reason_code, reason_detail, redaction_profile, summarization_profile, approval_requirement, effective_sensitivity, allowed_destination, denied_destination and policy_version.
Decision enum includes allow, allow_redacted, allow_summary_only, deny, approval_required and unavailable.

Reason codes include secret_blocked, cloud_blocked, external_worker_blocked, worker_not_allowed, runtime_not_allowed, model_scope_not_allowed, provider_location_blocked, write_not_allowed, unmatched_source_denied, approval_required, redaction_required, policy_error.
Decision contains enough information to explain why a block was removed from a prompt without exposing the block content.
Tests verify all decision enum values serialize and roundtrip.
anti_patterns: Do not return only true/false.
Do not put raw secret/context content into reason_detail.

tasks.title: Define DestinationContext contract for worker/runtime/model target
tasks.problem: Policy evaluation needs a normalized description of the destination: worker kind, runtime target, provider, model and tool/mode.
implementation_notes: DestinationContext should be built after worker/runtime selection preview or from the selected WorkerRuntimeSelectionDecision.
It should represent local-only, cloud, external worker and non-LLM tool targets clearly.
acceptance_criteria: DestinationContext includes worker_id, worker_kind, runtime_target_id, runtime_kind, provider_id, provider_location, model_id, model_scope, tool_id, task_kind, execution_mode, cloud_effective, external_effective, local_effective and requested_operation.
requested_operation distinguishes send_to_llm, send_to_worker, tool_read, tool_write, memory_write, artifact_store, repair_execute and review_only.
DestinationContext can be derived from WorkerRuntimeSelectionDecision and ExecutionEnvelope.
DestinationContext for local tool-only execution can have model_scope=none or local_tool_only.
Tests cover native local worker, OpenCode local, Hermes cloud/external, remote worker and local tool-only destination.
anti_patterns: Do not infer cloud/local only from model name.
Do not treat remote worker URL as safe local runtime.

tasks.title: Define source sensitivity and classification taxonomy
tasks.problem: Context policy needs consistent sensitivity values across files, CodeCompass, RAG, memory, artifacts, logs and user-provided context.
implementation_notes: If a sensitivity enum already exists, extend/reuse it instead of duplicating.
Use conservative default for unknown.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitivity taxonomy includes public, project_internal, customer_confidential, security_sensitive, secret, credential, regulated_data, generated_summary, unknown.
Each sensitivity maps to default allowed_model_scopes and cloud/external defaults.
secret and credential default to model_scope=none, send_allowed=false, write_allowed=false unless narrow tool-only policy says otherwise.
unknown defaults to deny for cloud/external destinations and allow only in explicit local policy if configured.
Tests verify default mapping for each sensitivity.
anti_patterns: Do not classify unknown as public.
Do not merge secret and project_internal.

tasks.title: Add policy precedence and merge rules
tasks.problem: Policies may come from global defaults, project, blueprint role, task, user override and emergency admin override. Precedence must be deterministic and fail-safe.
implementation_notes: Deny should normally dominate allow unless explicit approved override exists.
Task overrides should only narrow access by default; widening access should require permission/approval.
acceptance_criteria: Policy merge order is documented and implemented: system_default -> project -> blueprint_role -> task -> approved_override or equivalent.
Narrowing rules can be applied by lower scopes without special approval.
Widening cloud/external/secret access requires explicit override permission and audit.
Deny rules override allow rules unless approved_override explicitly references deny rule and allowed override class.
Merged policy includes provenance for every effective rule.
Tests cover project deny overriding blueprint allow, task narrowing, and approved override widening.
anti_patterns: Do not let user/task prompt widen access.
Do not allow last-write-wins for security rules.

tasks.title: Implement source matcher for paths, repositories, artifact types and source ids
tasks.problem: Rules need robust matching for local files, CodeCompass nodes, RAG chunks, artifacts, logs, memory and user prompt sources.
implementation_notes: Support glob patterns, source_type filters, repo/project ids, artifact kinds and metadata tags.
Avoid unsafe regex by default; if regex exists, validate/timeout it.
acceptance_criteria: Matcher supports glob path patterns such as src/**, docs/public/**, .env, **/*.pem, **/secrets/**.
Matcher supports source_type matching for codecompass_code, rag_chunk, memory, artifact, log and user_prompt.
Matcher supports metadata tags such as contains_credentials, generated, customer_data, security_rule, public_doc.
Most specific rule wins for classification, while deny precedence remains enforced during decision.
Invalid pattern is rejected during policy validation.
Tests cover path, source_type, tag and combined matches.
anti_patterns: Do not use substring checks for security-sensitive paths only.
Do not allow catastrophic regex patterns in policy.

tasks.title: Add built-in secret and credential detectors
tasks.problem: Path rules are not enough. The backend must detect common secrets and credentials in content and metadata before context reaches a model or external worker.
implementation_notes: Start with deterministic detectors: private key blocks, Bearer tokens, API keys, .env assignments, DB URLs with password, SSH keys, JWT-like tokens.
Detection should classify and optionally redact; classification must not require sending content to LLM.
acceptance_criteria: Detector flags PEM private key blocks as credential/secret.
Detector flags Authorization Bearer tokens as credential/secret.
Detector flags .env-style SECRET=, TOKEN=, API_KEY=, PASSWORD= assignments.
Detector flags database URLs with embedded password.
Detector flags SSH private key material.
Redaction replaces detected value with stable marker and preserves debugging structure.
Tests prove detected secrets are denied for all LLM destinations by default.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on file names only for secret detection.
Do not log raw detected secret in test failure messages or decisions.

tasks.title: Classify CodeCompass nodes and edges with sensitivity metadata
tasks.problem: CodeCompass/RAG may retrieve code, graph edges, dependency metadata and architectural relationships. Each returned block needs sensitivity and policy metadata before being used.
implementation_notes: Classify by source path, node type, tags and content detector.
Graph edges can leak architecture; classify them too.
acceptance_criteria: CodeCompass file/code nodes carry source_type, path, sensitivity, source_tags and policy_source_ref metadata.
CodeCompass graph edge/context blocks carry sensitivity based on source nodes and graph metadata.
Files under secrets/config/env patterns are classified secret/security_sensitive before retrieval output is eligible for prompt assembly.
Architecture/security policy documents can be classified security_sensitive or project_internal according to rules.
Tests prove CodeCompass retrieval for cloud destination excludes secret/config nodes and records reason codes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat graph metadata as harmless by default.
Do not classify all code as cloud_allowed.

tasks.title: Classify memory and artifact sources before reuse
tasks.problem: Result memory and artifacts can contain summarized code, command output, logs or accidental secrets. Reusing them as context requires policy classification too.
implementation_notes: Memory/artifact metadata should include originating sensitivity and provider/worker provenance.
Generated summary does not automatically become public.
acceptance_criteria: Memory entries include or derive sensitivity, source_refs, provider_provenance and approved_scope metadata.
Artifact refs include artifact_kind, source_worker, source_runtime, content_hash and sensitivity metadata for policy evaluation.
Generated summaries inherit the maximum sensitivity of their source unless explicitly downgraded by approved sanitizer.
Cloud/external destinations cannot receive memory/artifact context derived from secret/security_sensitive sources by default.
Tests prove generated summary from secret source remains blocked for cloud model.
anti_patterns: Do not treat summaries as safe just because they are short.
Do not forget provenance when storing memory.

tasks.title: Add policy linting and conflict detection
tasks.problem: User-configurable policies can conflict or create unsafe illusions. The backend should lint policies before saving/enabling them.
implementation_notes: Lint should produce warnings/errors with target rule ids.
Critical lint errors block save/activation.
acceptance_criteria: Linter detects unreachable rules due to earlier deny precedence.
Linter detects secret/cloud allow conflicts.
Linter detects write_allowed rules without approval requirement for mutation-capable sources.
Linter detects overly broad patterns like ** with cloud_allowed=true and sensitivity not constrained.
Linter detects unknown worker/runtime/model scope references.
Lint result has severity error/warning/info, rule_id and suggested_fix.
test_requirements: Policy lint tests for each conflict type.
anti_patterns: Do not let invalid policy activate because UI created it.
Do not bury lint errors in server logs only.

tasks.title: Implement can_send_context_block decision engine
tasks.problem: The central enforcement point must evaluate a single context block against the destination and policy.
implementation_notes: Function signature should accept block metadata, DestinationContext, operation and merged policy.
Content may be passed only for redaction/classification, never required for already-classified decisions.
acceptance_criteria: can_send_context_block returns ContextBlockAccessDecision for allow, allow_redacted, allow_summary_only, deny and approval_required.
Decision requires source rule allow AND worker allow AND runtime allow AND model/provider allow.
Any explicit deny wins unless an approved override applies.
Cloud destination with cloud_allowed=false returns deny/cloud_blocked.
External worker destination with external_worker_allowed=false returns deny/external_worker_blocked.
Secret/credential block to any LLM destination returns deny/secret_blocked by default.
Local tool-only destination can be allowed for selected secret read only when policy explicitly allows local_tool_only and operation is not send_to_llm.
test_requirements: Decision matrix tests for native local, OpenCode local, Hermes cloud, remote worker and local tool-only.
anti_patterns: Do not shortcut allow when worker is local; still check sensitivity and operation.
Do not allow LLM access to secrets because runtime is local.

tasks.title: Implement context redaction and summary-only transforms
tasks.problem: Some blocks may be allowed only as redacted or summarized context. That must be deterministic and policy-controlled.
implementation_notes: Redaction should be deterministic and local.
Summary-only for sensitive data should not call cloud LLM; use local model or deterministic metadata summary depending on policy.
acceptance_criteria: Redaction transform removes detected secrets and marks redaction_applied=true.
Summary-only transform can produce metadata-only summary without raw sensitive content.
Transform result includes transform_type, source_hash, output_hash, policy_rule_id and residual_sensitivity.
If summary transform requires model call, provider must be allowed by same context policy.
Transform failure downgrades to deny, not raw allow.
Tests prove secret value is not present after redaction and that failed transform denies.
anti_patterns: Do not send raw block to cloud summarizer to make it safe for cloud.
Do not treat redaction as complete without residual sensitivity metadata.

tasks.title: Add write access decision engine
tasks.problem: Read/send policy is not enough. Workers/tools may write files, memory, artifacts or runtime state, and writes need separate checks.
implementation_notes: Write decisions should check target path/source, worker/runtime, tool, approval and operation type.
Local workers may read broadly but write only through explicit allowed paths/tools.
acceptance_criteria: can_write_context_target or equivalent function exists and returns structured decision.
Write to source classified secret/credential is denied by default.
Write to project code requires write_allowed=true, tool allowed and approval when policy requires mutation approval.
Memory write requires memory_write policy and sensitivity checks.
Artifact store write is allowed only for artifact kinds permitted by task/worker policy.
Tests prove local worker cannot write secrets/config just because it can read project files.
anti_patterns: Do not couple read_allowed=true to write_allowed=true.
Do not let patch apply bypass context access write policy.

tasks.title: Add approval override flow for data-boundary exceptions
tasks.problem: Sometimes an operator may need a narrow exception. The backend needs scoped, auditable approval override instead of broad config disablement.
implementation_notes: Overrides should be narrow: source rule, destination, operation, time, task/goal, approver.
Some classes such as credentials-to-cloud may remain non-overridable unless system policy allows.
acceptance_criteria: Override model includes override_id, approved_by, expires_at, source_scope, destination_scope, operation_scope, reason, max_sensitivity, non_overridable_flags and audit_ref.
Override cannot broaden beyond admin/system maximum policy.
Expired override is ignored and returns approval_required or deny.
Override use is recorded in ContextBlockAccessDecision and audit.
Tests cover valid narrow override, expired override and forbidden override class.
anti_patterns: Do not add a global disable data policy switch.
Do not allow overrides without reason/audit/expiry.

tasks.title: Make policy decisions deterministic and cacheable by hash
tasks.problem: For audit and repeatability, the same block/destination/policy should produce the same decision and hash.
implementation_notes: Hash inputs should exclude unstable timestamps but include policy version, block hash and destination.
acceptance_criteria: Decision includes decision_hash computed from policy version, block metadata/hash, destination context and operation.
Same input produces same decision_hash.
Changing policy rule changes decision_hash.
Changing destination from local to cloud changes decision_hash and likely decision.
Decision cache never returns allow for a different destination or newer policy version.
anti_patterns: Do not cache context decision only by block id.
Do not ignore destination/model in cache key.

tasks.title: Filter CodeCompass/RAG retrieval before prompt assembly
tasks.problem: CodeCompass/RAG must not return forbidden blocks to prompt assembly and hope later code removes them. Filtering must happen as part of retrieval/context bundle building for the destination.
implementation_notes: ContextBundleService should accept DestinationContext or WorkerRuntimeSelectionDecision before selecting final blocks.
CodeCompass may still rank forbidden blocks internally, but final selected context must exclude/transform them.
acceptance_criteria: ContextBundleService can build destination-specific context bundles.
For cloud/external destinations, forbidden CodeCompass/RAG blocks are excluded or transformed according to policy before prompt_text is assembled.
Bundle metadata includes included_blocks, denied_blocks, redacted_blocks, summarized_blocks and reason codes.
If all required context is denied, bundle status is degraded/context_unavailable instead of ungrounded prompt.
Tests prove .env/secrets/config blocks are never included in cloud prompt even if retrieval ranks them highest.
anti_patterns: Do not assemble full prompt and then redact it best-effort.
Do not continue with empty context without warning when task requires grounding.

tasks.title: Add required_context_class and fallback behavior
tasks.problem: Some tasks require private/local context. If cloud/external worker cannot receive it, the system should choose another worker or degrade, not send forbidden data.
implementation_notes: Tie context policy into worker selection: if required context cannot go to a worker, reject that worker candidate.
Required context class examples: public_only, project_internal_required, security_sensitive_required, secret_tool_access_required.
acceptance_criteria: Task/repair/blueprint config can declare required_context_class.
WorkerRuntimeSelectionService rejects candidates that cannot receive required context class.
If local worker can receive required context but cloud worker cannot, automatic selection prefers/chooses local worker.
If no worker can receive required context, result is degraded/no_context_eligible_worker.
Tests cover cloud rejected due to required project_internal context and local selected instead.
test_requirements: Worker selection plus context class tests.
anti_patterns: Do not choose cloud worker and then starve it of required context without warning.
Do not downgrade required context class silently.

tasks.title: Propagate ContextAccessDecision metadata into ContextEnvelope
tasks.problem: ExecutionEnvelope and Worker need to know what context was included/denied/transformed and why.
implementation_notes: Keep decision details bounded and content-free.
ContextEnvelope should include policy version and decision hashes.
acceptance_criteria: ContextEnvelope includes access_policy_id, access_policy_version, destination_context_hash, block_decisions, denied_block_count, redacted_block_count, summarized_block_count and context_access_summary.
Each included block references its access decision hash.
Denied block metadata does not contain raw content.
ExecutionEnvelope includes context_access_summary for trace/audit.
Tests verify ContextEnvelope contains decisions for included, denied and redacted blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not lose policy decision details once prompt is assembled.
Do not include denied raw content in envelope metadata.

tasks.title: Add policy-aware prompt assembly guard
tasks.problem: Even if retrieval filters context, prompt assembly should refuse to assemble prompts from blocks without allow/transform decisions for the selected destination.
implementation_notes: This is defense in depth against bypasses.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt assembly requires DestinationContext and ContextEnvelope with access decisions.
Prompt assembly rejects any block without allow/allow_redacted/allow_summary_only decision for the same destination hash.
Prompt assembly rejects stale decisions from different policy version or destination.
Prompt assembly never includes raw denied blocks.
Tests prove manually injected block without decision is rejected.
anti_patterns: Do not allow raw block list prompt assembly for worker/model calls.
Do not trust caller to already filtered context.

tasks.title: Add policy-aware retrieval diagnostics
tasks.problem: Operators need to understand why a worker got less context than expected or why no context was available.
implementation_notes: Diagnostics should be safe to show: no denied content, only counts and reason codes.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics expose total_retrieved, included, denied, redacted, summarized and unavailable counts.
Diagnostics group denied counts by reason_code and source_type.
Diagnostics include selected worker/runtime/model destination summary.
Diagnostics include top policy rules responsible for denies/redactions.
Diagnostics do not expose raw denied content.
test_requirements: Diagnostics tests for denied cloud context and redacted secret context.
anti_patterns: Do not include secret path/content in user-facing diagnostics unless permission allows path disclosure.
Do not just show 'context empty' without reasons.

tasks.title: Integrate context policy with WorkerRuntimeSelectionService
tasks.problem: Worker/runtime selection must account for whether required context may be sent to the worker/model/runtime.
implementation_notes: Evaluate candidate destinations before final selection.
Reject candidates that cannot receive required context or require disallowed cloud.
acceptance_criteria: Worker candidate evaluation includes context_eligibility_status.
Candidates are rejected when required context is denied by policy.
Candidates that receive only summary/redacted context are marked degraded/limited_context unless task allows it.
Selection decision includes context rejection reasons per worker/runtime candidate.
Tests prove Hermes/cloud candidate rejected while native local worker selected for project_internal_required task.
anti_patterns: Do not select a worker first and only later discover it cannot receive context.
Do not send context to test eligibility.

tasks.title: Enforce policy before external/cloud LLM provider calls
tasks.problem: Any external/cloud model call must be blocked unless every prompt/context block is allowed for that provider/model scope.
implementation_notes: Model provider call helper should require ContextEnvelope and DestinationContext.
Policy guard should run before request payload is built/sent.
acceptance_criteria: Cloud provider call is denied if prompt/context contains any block without allow/allow_redacted/allow_summary_only decision for that cloud provider destination.
Provider call guard rejects stale decisions from different destination/model scope.
Provider call trace records context_access_summary and policy_version.
Provider call denial occurs before network request is made.
Tests monkeypatch provider transport and verify it is not called when secret/project_internal block is denied.
test_requirements: Provider call guard tests with no-network assertion.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on frontend warning for cloud calls.
Do not let provider wrapper accept raw prompt strings for governed worker calls.

tasks.title: Enforce policy before external worker calls such as Hermes/OpenCode remote
tasks.problem: External worker calls can leak context even if no direct cloud model provider is called by Ananta, because the worker runtime may use cloud internally.
implementation_notes: DestinationContext must include external_effective/cloud_effective based on worker runtime metadata and declared model/provider behind it.
Remote OpenCode/Hermes should be treated as external unless runtime target is validated local/private.
acceptance_criteria: Hermes/external worker adapter refuses context blocks not allowed for external worker destination.
Remote OpenCode worker refuses project_internal/secret context unless policy and runtime validation allow it.
Worker call denial happens before HTTP/process invocation.
Adapter result includes context_access_summary and denied reason codes.
Tests verify remote Hermes does not receive forbidden project/internal/secret context.
anti_patterns: Do not assume OpenCode is local just because worker_kind=opencode.
Do not trust external worker to enforce Ananta context policy itself.

tasks.title: Enforce policy before tool read/write operations
tasks.problem: Tools can read or modify local files/secrets independent of model prompts. ToolInvocationEnvelope must check read/write source policy.
implementation_notes: Use requested_operation tool_read/tool_write and target path/source classification.
Shell commands may need conservative path extraction; unknown side-effect means approval/deny.
acceptance_criteria: File read tool checks source policy before reading local file into worker/model context.
File write/patch apply checks write policy before mutation.
Shell command that appears to read .env/private key/secret path is denied or requires explicit local_tool_only approval according to policy.
Shell command that appears to write outside allowed workspace is denied.
Tool invocation denial occurs before subprocess/file operation.
Tests prove local worker cannot read .env into LLM context and cannot write secret file by default.
anti_patterns: Do not treat shell commands as opaque safe strings.
Do not let local tools bypass data-boundary policy.

tasks.title: Enforce policy before memory write and memory retrieval
tasks.problem: Memory can leak sensitive information later to different workers/models. Memory write and retrieval must obey data-boundary policy.
implementation_notes: Memory entries should store sensitivity and allowed scopes.
Retrieval should filter memory entries for destination before adding to context bundle.
acceptance_criteria: Memory write evaluates content/source sensitivity and policy before persist.
Secret/security-sensitive memory is denied or stored only as local_tool_only/redacted according to policy.
Memory retrieval filters entries by DestinationContext before returning chunks.
Generated memory from cloud model output carries provider provenance and trust/sensitivity metadata.
Tests prove memory created from secret source is not retrieved for cloud worker.
anti_patterns: Do not make memory a laundering path from secret to cloud.
Do not retrieve all project memory and filter later in prompt assembly only.

tasks.title: Persist ContextAccessPolicy at project and blueprint role level
tasks.problem: Users need to configure data-boundary policy persistently for project, blueprint role and task flows.
implementation_notes: Use a dedicated table if possible. BlueprintRoleDB.config may reference policy id or embed role-level overrides.
Keep history/versioning.
acceptance_criteria: ContextAccessPolicyDB or equivalent persistent model exists with project_id, policy_id, version, scope, enabled, policy_json, lint_status, created_by, updated_by, created_at, updated_at.
Repository supports create, update, get active by project/scope, list versions and disable policy.
BlueprintRoleDB.config can reference context_access_policy_id and/or role-level override rules.
Policy save validates schema and lint errors before activation.
Tests prove policy persists across DB session and can be retrieved by project/role.
anti_patterns: Do not store only in frontend local state.
Do not overwrite policy without version/history.

tasks.title: Add Context Policy API endpoints
tasks.problem: Frontend needs validated APIs to list, edit, lint, preview and activate context access policies.
implementation_notes: Endpoints should never return raw secrets from matched sources.
Preview should use synthetic or selected source metadata, not raw secret content.
acceptance_criteria: API supports list policies by project/scope.
API supports get policy version.
API supports create/update draft policy.
API supports lint policy and returns structured errors/warnings.
API supports activate policy only when validation passes and caller authorized.
API supports decision preview for sample source metadata + destination context.
API supports effective policy read model after merge/precedence.
Tests cover create, lint, activate, preview and unauthorized access.
anti_patterns: Do not let frontend send invalid policy directly into active config.
Do not expose raw file content in decision preview by default.

tasks.title: Add policy import/export and blueprint bundle integration
tasks.problem: Teams/blueprints must carry context access policy safely across export/import without losing data-boundary rules.
implementation_notes: Import should lint before activation and support dry-run diff.
acceptance_criteria: Blueprint/team bundle export includes context access policies referenced by roles or project profile.
Bundle import validates and lints policies before activation.
Import dry-run reports policy additions, changes, conflicts and blocked rules.
Policy ids are remapped safely if needed while preserving references from BlueprintRoleDB.config.
Tests cover export/import roundtrip and invalid imported policy.
anti_patterns: Do not strip policy rules during bundle export.
Do not auto-activate imported unsafe policies.

tasks.title: Add default policy templates
tasks.problem: Users need safe defaults and templates instead of building data-boundary policy from scratch.
implementation_notes: Templates should be conservative and explain tradeoffs.
Frontend will use these as presets.
acceptance_criteria: Templates include strict_local_only, local_code_cloud_docs, external_analysis_public_only, cloud_allowed_sanitized_docs, enterprise_strict_no_cloud and dev_experimental_with_approval.
Every template validates and lints clean or with documented warnings.
Templates include descriptions, intended use, risk level and what is denied.
strict_local_only denies all cloud/external model access to project code by default.
external_analysis_public_only allows only public/docs context to external/cloud workers.
Tests verify key allow/deny decisions for each template.
anti_patterns: Do not ship permissive cloud-all template as default.
Do not name a risky template 'safe'.

tasks.title: Add migration/backfill for existing config fields
tasks.problem: Existing fields such as preferred_backend, cloud_allowed, risk_profile and worker_roles must map into the new policy model without breaking current behavior.
implementation_notes: Migration should be conservative: if uncertain, deny cloud/external sensitive context.
Backfill should not overwrite explicit user policies.
acceptance_criteria: Migration creates default ContextAccessPolicy for projects/roles without one.
Existing cloud_allowed=false maps to deny cloud/external for project_internal and stricter sensitivities.
Existing preferred_backend maps to worker selection but not context allow by itself.
Existing risk_profile strict maps to conservative template.
Migration is idempotent.
Tests cover migration from empty config, strict config and cloud-allowed config.
anti_patterns: Do not infer cloud context permission from preferred_backend alone.
Do not create permissive policy when migration lacks information.

tasks.title: Add context access decisions to TraceBundle and audit events
tasks.problem: Every model/worker/tool context decision must be auditable without exposing sensitive content.
implementation_notes: Trace should include counts and hashes; audit should record sensitive boundary crossings/denials.
acceptance_criteria: TraceBundle includes context_access_policy_id, policy_version, destination_context_hash, included_count, denied_count, redacted_count, summarized_count and decision_hashes/ref.

Audit events include context_access_evaluated, context_block_denied, context_block_redacted, context_policy_override_used, context_sent_to_external_worker, context_sent_to_cloud_provider where applicable.
Audit event for cloud/external send includes worker_kind, runtime_kind, provider_id/model_scope and counts by sensitivity, not raw content.
Denied secret content is not logged.
Tests verify audit emitted and redacted.
anti_patterns: Do not store raw prompt/context in audit by default.
Do not audit only successful sends; denials matter.

tasks.title: Add security regression suite for data-boundary bypasses
tasks.problem: Data-boundary policy is security-critical and needs focused regression tests.
implementation_notes: Use fake workers/providers; assert no transport called when denied.
acceptance_criteria: Suite proves .env is never sent to cloud model.
Suite proves private key block is denied for local LLM by default.
Suite proves native local tool-only can be allowed to inspect a secret only with explicit policy and no LLM send.
Suite proves remote worker cannot receive project_internal context when external_worker_allowed=false.
Suite proves prompt assembly rejects blocks without destination-matching decision.
Suite proves memory cannot launder secret-derived summary to cloud.
Suite proves write policy blocks secret/config mutation by default.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on happy-path policy tests.
Do not require live cloud provider for security tests.

tasks.title: Add system health and diagnostics for context access policy
tasks.problem: Operators need to see if context access policy is active, valid, degraded or bypassed.
implementation_notes: Diagnostics should show policy coverage and unsafe defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Health includes context_access_policy_enabled, active_policy_id, policy_version, lint_status, last_decision_error, bypass_mode_enabled=false/true and coverage_summary.
Diagnostics warn when no active project policy exists and default policy is being used.
Diagnostics warn when cloud providers are configured but no explicit cloud rules exist.
Diagnostics list template/policy source without secrets.
Tests cover healthy, missing policy and lint error states.
anti_patterns: Do not silently run without policy when user expects enforcement.
Do not expose sensitive rules/content in health output.

tasks.title: Add performance tests for policy evaluation at retrieval scale
tasks.problem: Policy must run over many retrieved blocks without making RAG unusably slow.
implementation_notes: Use synthetic blocks and policies.
Measure enough to catch pathological behavior, not micro-optimize prematurely.
acceptance_criteria: Policy evaluation handles at least 1000 synthetic blocks within documented test threshold.
Decision caching reduces repeated evaluation cost for same block/destination/policy.
Invalid or broad patterns do not cause pathological runtime.
Performance test is optional/marked if environment variability is high.
anti_patterns: Do not call expensive content detectors repeatedly for unchanged block hash.
Do not evaluate regex without safeguards.

tasks.title: Write backend architecture and operator docs
tasks.problem: This policy layer will be complex. Backend developers/operators need clear docs to avoid unsafe bypasses.
implementation_notes: Docs should include diagrams and example policies.
Clearly distinguish read, send_to_llm, tool_read, tool_write and memory_write.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc explains source classification, DestinationContext, decision engine, transformations, retrieval integration and enforcement points.
Operator doc includes safe templates and examples for local-only, cloud-docs-only and external-analysis-only configurations.
Docs explicitly state secrets are denied to LLMs by default, including local LLMs, unless a narrow policy says otherwise.
Docs list APIs and test commands.
Docs explain how policy interacts with worker/runtime selection and deterministic repair.
test_requirements: Docs examples validate against policy schema when practical.
anti_patterns: Do not document planned enforcement as implemented until tests exist.
Do not hide default-deny behavior.

definition_of_done_global: ContextAccessPolicy schema exists and validates source-to-destination rules.
Every context block receives an allow/redact/summarize/deny/approval decision before prompt assembly.
CodeCompass/RAG/context bundles filter by destination before prompt assembly.
Prompt assembly rejects blocks without valid destination-matching access decisions.
Cloud/external provider and worker calls are blocked before network/process invocation when context is not allowed.
Tool read/write and memory write/retrieval obey separate read/write/data-boundary policy.
Policies persist by project/role, can be linted, versioned, imported/exported and previewed through API.
Trace/audit/diagnostics explain included, denied, redacted and summarized blocks without leaking content.
Security regression tests prove secrets and project-internal context cannot leak to unauthorized workers/models.
summary_notes: This backend track is a hard security prerequisite for safe worker/runtime/model selection.
The frontend may configure policies, but backend enforcement is authoritative.
The most important rule: CodeCompass/RAG must provide the best permitted context for the selected worker/runtime/model, not simply the best context overall.
